name: WRATH: Aeon of Ruin VR - Brutal Edition

reviews.review_description:
A high-octane boomer shooter that's blistering fast and brutally difficult like the games of yesteryears.

Flat2VR excels at taking flat-screen cult and underappreciated titles before updating them for the VR crowd.

Anyone who's played the original Doom or Quake games will know what to expect here.
Explore maps, looking for keys to further open up areas, all whilst battling demons with a selection of different weapons.

But it's more than a simple flat to VR port.
Team Beef has taken the core Wraith game; added a new slide and bullet time mechanic.
Followed with motion control for the melee weapons, dual wielding and a new flick reload– all things that can only really be done in VR with justice.
Once you start dual-wielding a pistol/shotgun combo and flick reloading.
Or simultaneously shooting two separate enemies charging at you from two different angles, you will understand what I mean. 

What was originally a solid title is now a must buy in VR.
A fully fledged title that will take at least 15 hours before they see the end credits.

developer_response.body: Hey Burntbreadman, thank you for playing and for the detailed review! We’re thrilled you’re enjoying the new VR-specific mechanics like the flick reload and dual-wielding. Bringing that 'brutally difficult' retro feel to VR was the goal, and it’s awesome to see it resonating with the community!

reviews.review_description:
This isn’t usually my type of game...but WOW, I got hooked.

Once you start learning the mechanics, it just clicks. And, then it’s nonstop chaos. Zombies coming at you, slashing, shooting, going full brutal mode…it’s insanely satisfying.

It’s fast, it’s intense, and it’s just straight-up FUN.

If you want something you can jump into and lose hours in without even realizing it, this is it.

Trust me…you’re not going to want to put it down.

developer_response.body: Hey Natafaha9, hearing that our game hooked someone who doesn't usually play this type of game is the best compliment we could get! Thrilled you're enjoying the nonstop chaos. Welcome to the Ruins!

reviews.review_title: One of the best games on Quest
reviews.review_description:
This is a full fledge game . Not some tech demo.

Very good game . I was never into the flat version. I played the original team beef mod and didn’t like it .

But once I played this version I couldn’t stop playing it. The weapon handling is amazing. Controls are smooth . Kills are satisfying

Please bring more boomer shooter to VR.

Someone do Ion Fury

Wrath is not only old timey in it's gameplay, story and beautiful artistic graphics. It's also old timey in the most important way.. which is how nostalgic the Fun you get with wrath is! Everything is earned, no hand holding. The game is pretty long but so sweet where you always crave more. The artstyle is amazing! Gameplay reminds me heavily of the Quake/Doom games. Can't help but recommend!💯

developer_response.body: Hey KindGentlemanThatisntKind, thank you for the review! Super glad hear that the nostalgic factor hit home leaving you wanting more. Have fun!

reviews.review_description: I have never played the original Wrath, but I have played enough Doom to tell you that this game is awesome. You hack, you slash, you shoot, you dash. It's all the fun you want if you love the Doom series. I love the aesthetics, and I love the level design.I mean, it is ported by the best team that there is: Dr. Beef team. It's the first official Doom-like game on the MetaQuest, and I think that it is a very well-made representation of the series. There isn't anything to complain about, so I'm sure you're gonna enjoy this game.

developer_response.body: Hey Majed6, thank you so much for playing! As a team that started with unofficial ports of the classics, bringing WRATH to VR as an official release has been an incredible journey and we’re thrilled to hear that the vision we had for WRATH translated well to a Doom fan!

reviews.review_title: Remarkable retro Quake like FPS shooter
reviews.review_description: If you are/were a Quake fan, you are going to love being inside this classic Retro style, Quake like FPS shooter.  The scale and variety of the environments is breath taking in VR.  The movements, weapon handling, weapon and enemy variety and combat feel so natural, the spacial sound design allows you to hear directionally when enemies spawn in and the rockin sound track just adds to the experience, pair all this with native bhaptics support and you actully feel like you are in the game.  This was a game that went on my wishlist the day it was announced and does not disappoint don't sleep on this one highly recommended.

reviews.review_title: Just wish it had different music
reviews.review_description: Bought the flat game when I heard this was coming out but didn’t play it. Not to knock that game but the vr trailer made this games music seem like doom (heavy metal) and so far it’s just ambient. Which is fine, but the metal would take this game to a new level. Maybe add an alternate sound track? Only other things and thy are minor is you press a button to do switches.  With all the work to make everything intuitive in vr, that feels out of place but I’m guessing there is a reason. Going up stairs also feels janky. All in all team beef and flat2vr got another winner here though and I’ll buy pretty much whatever keeps them making vr ports.

Okay so I pre-ordered on a whim I have never played the flat screen nor have I played a game by either these devs. 
Honestly it seemed incredible. 

The gameplay and VR fun factor deserve 4-5 stars. 
Unfortunately, there seems to be bug stopping me from completing the game. 

I have completed all 5 worlds but only collect 4/5 relics. When I go to the one I am missing and use the pathfinder it points me to the portal to leave. I actually think I already collected it and it didn't register. I can't even really find many more enemies in that world and relic is no where to be found. I think it is glitched out and I cannot finish that world and therefore I cannot finish the game. 

I have put several hours into it and hoping to complete it. I liked it and was initially happy I invested day one. 
If the dev responds and is able to help I'll rewrite an actual review.

developer_response.body:
Hi J_StuntCat_K, very glad to hear you are enjoying the game. We're keen to solve any issues. If you could provide us with your save games we'll be more than happy to investigate and see what's gone wrong. You can find the save games on your Quest headset at Android/data/com.teambeef.wrathvr/files (connected to your computer). If you can zip up the contents and send to us on the official bug board: https://wrath-labs.feedbear.com/boards/livebuild we'll get you back up and running. 

BTW there are actually 3 Worlds each with 5 levels, so you have a lot of fun waiting ahead! 2nd and 3rd Worlds are a completely different aesthetic

reviews.review_title: Old school shooter - but in full VR!
reviews.review_description:
I've been playing video games for a while and I have some very found memories of the first FPSs (Wolfenstein 3D, Doom, Hexen, Duke, ...). Wrath: Aeon of Ruin VR is a dream come true: every bit the true old school shooter, but with a full VR experience.
Wrath: Aeon of Ruin really captures the essence of those first shooters (fast, punchy, sometimes brutally difficult, etc) as well as a fantastic sort of pixelated 3d, which brings me back to my young adult years. But this VR version is so much more: it's a subtile alchemy which manages to keep all the qualities of the shooter while offering a fantastic VR autonomous experience.
If you are, like me, nostalgic of those good old shooters, I can't recommend this title enough: you can reconnect with what made (and still make) those shooters so fun; but this time, you're really in the game and can make the most of what VR brings to video games.
And if you never played those old shooters; well, you're in for a treat as you'll be able to discover the best of what they have to offer.. in VR!

I am 53 so I need easy mode for my slower reaction speed. The game reminds me of quake but in VR. Lots of enemies, guns and helpful artifacts, ammo and health pickups. It even has a handy navigation trail that can be displayed at any time. Quick saves are skulls that you can pick up and use anywhere. The levels are huge so I go from shrine to shrine and treat them like mini levels. I also use the Quest game optimized and change the resolution scale to 150 percent and it looks crisp and clean. Altogether it is great fun. Would love some mods like doom and quake to add to the games lifespan, I just want more. :)

I constantly catch myself in awe at how at immersive it is to be able be literally inside a DOOM-like game. The game play is very smooth and the controls are very intuitive. I'm also astounded at how large the maps are. Extremely addicting game. If you loved playing DOOM back in the day you owe it to yourself to pick this game up. It's a no brainer.

This is a no-nonsense, high-intensity shooter that knows exactly what it is and doesn’t waste time trying to appeal to everyone. If you’re looking for hand-holding or modern shortcuts, this isn’t it. The gameplay is built around fast movement, tough enemies, and classic exploration where you’re hunting for keys and unlocking new areas while constantly under pressure, and the difficulty expects you to actually improve rather than coast through. Where it really stands out is in VR, because this isn’t just a basic port. The dual-wielding, physical reload mechanics, and melee interactions fundamentally change how combat feels, especially when you’re dealing with enemies coming at you from multiple directions at once. It leans heavily into that old-school Doom and Quake style, both in pacing and design, but it doesn’t feel outdated. It feels deliberate, with a strong visual style and tight level design that keeps you engaged for the long haul. Bottom line, it’s demanding, fast, and rewarding, and if you’re willing to put in the effort, it absolutely delivers.

reviews.review_title: A Fun, Tough, Exciting VR Boomer Shooter
reviews.review_description: This one took me by suprise! I was never big on Boomer Shooters flat. Much like many other VR games this one got it's hooks in deep and has me loving the genre in VR.  Stands to be my default vacation stress relief game. You get 15 levels but with searching for secrets the game will have you logging well upwards of 20 hours of action to fully complete. The flick reloading and blade slides are just the start of the goodness. Searching out secrets while dodging projectiles and incoming hordes is just the right level of replayable fun.  I also love that there are moments of sheer verticality where you can jump without taking damage into caverns and into firefights. Don't wait! See what I'm going on about and get in this one pronto!

VR needs more games like this. Full, quality games that run flawlessly and are a blast to play. 

This is a modern-classic game. It's heavily inspired by shooters like Doom and Quake, and there is very minimal story but it's enough to get you going. The game is fast, it's hard, it feels great to play (no teleport though which is an immediate no buy for some), and I recommend this to anyone who enjoys VR shooters or boomer shooters as this genre is called. The classic graphics look nice, though some environments do feel repetitive. Overall, it's a great game and a great price at $20. Support VR devs who bring quality titles to the Quest platform!

Remember those early old school shooters like doom, quakes, duke... This is one of those old time goodies that now has a polished VR conversion with full VR control and haptics.  The game is of an older graphic style but it runs very well and in VR with skeletons, monsters, demons coming at you fast and furious it is easy to get lost in the moment and down right freaky at times.  Definitely rec this for those who love old school shooters.  Also the game is down right hard and definitely will get plenty of play time.

I lost 40 minutes of progress and merely laughed because I'm having so much fun. I'd never heard of this game but it feels like stepping in Unreal (classic) and Quake and old titles like that but so intense! There's a slider to slow down your movement speed, and slow motion for if things get too intense - i have like 45 saves i could use but I keep hoarding them so I'm only on like the 4th level but I keep losing progress so it's my own fault. I think the gesture reloading could use some tweaking i find myself having to really flick the controller to pull it off and I end up panic shaking the controller like a mad man in intense situations. But this is seriously fun and nostalgic. I'm having a great time! Fantastic maps, fantastic gameplay, highly recommend and if you think the movement is fast wait till you start bunny hopping and dashing with the swords to get around!

reviews.review_title: A brutal homage to boomer shooters
reviews.review_description:
VR shooters come and go, but Wrath: Aeon of Ruin VR will stick with you. Fantastic weapons that you cater to your playstyle, both big open maps and winding mazes, along with lots of frantic combat... It's a love letter to both the boomer shooter games of the past and a step towards the future.

Really can't recommend WAOR VR enough. If you love old-school shooters and have a VR headset, you should definitely check this one out.

Old school dungeon crawler that goes way too hard. Massive maps that are constantly throwing new enemies and weapons at you. Sometimes the vibe of the map gave some Dark Souls energy.
I wish there was some sort of weapon customization but I believe the game is meant to be as it is. Still very fun!

This game plays incredibly well and team beef did an amazing job porting this to VR. The action is brutal and fast paced. The addition of some vr specific actions such as motion reloading, sliding and blocking projectiles really brings up the immersion. This game is very challenging even on the easier difficulty, but never unfair. If you've played games like quake or doom take yourself back to that time and always keep moving! The weapon wheels and item selects are also snappy and work great. I definitely would recommend this game.

This is very cool! Us older gamers are going to immediately appreciate the throwback to the high action gaming we grew up on. I never played the flat screen version, but VR brings a magical touch to any game. If you are a fan of boomer shooters, you're gonna love it. Heck, if you like action, puzzles, monsters, guns, blades, and incredibly deep voice actors that sound like they are in your actual head, you'll enjoy it.

developer_response.body: Hey SaltyDadda, glad we could bring back some of that nostalgia for you! Thank you for playing and for writing us a review.

reviews.review_description: Wrath perfectly captures the frantic spirit of early Doom and evolves it for the VR era.The game strikes a brilliant balance between "run-and-gun" chaos and deliberate exploration, tucked away inside a hauntingly beautiful world. Every secret found feels like a triumph. Team Beef has delivered a must-play experience that is as immersive as it is visceral.

reviews.review_title: One of the most fun boomer shooters
reviews.review_description:
This game is one of the most fun boomer shooters of all time. The movement mechanics allow for some intense high speed traversal, combat positioning and evasion. 

You can chain the blade dash by cycling between left and right charges. Once you get the timing down, you can cover large distances very quickly. You can’t charge the blades while sliding, but there is a slide cancel mechanic (up on right joystick before slide ends). As soon as the slide is canceled you can start charging the blade dash again. 

I recommend cranking the movement speed slider to max. I actually almost lost balance a couple times, which hasn’t happened to me in VR in years.

Combine all this with the excellent VR implementation, such as the flick reloading, you get the best old and new school gaming.


This is a future classic. Highly recommended.

reviews.review_title: Retro to VR at its finest!!!
reviews.review_description: This is definitely the brutal edition. This game is nostalgic for those of us who are around during the original ages of doom and Wolfenstein and Duke Nukem. Wrath is hands down the best VR edition of the classic first person shooter games from back in the day. You don't have to have played the flat version to appreciate and love this game. You get to dual wield, you get to collect weapons, you have the power of slo-mo, and what feels to be an unending flow of enemies to blast away. Each area has its own secrets and Relics to collect as well as unique enemies to take down. Don't wait for a sale on this game, this game truly is worth every penny and the developers deserve it completely. Flat2VR and Team Beef are incredible and I look forward to what they bring out next. Until then I think I'm hooked on this game. Like I said don't wait grab it now and enjoy every moment of it. You will not regret it.

I haven't been playing for long, just a couple of levels in, but so far this is one of the most fun games I've played on the quest. It's hard to believe that the game wasn't originally made for VR. Flat2vr studios and Team Beef have done an amazing job of converting it. If you are a fan of the original on pc or Doom and Quake, I highly recommend this.

Often VR games can be shorter experiences with smaller levels than your typical flat game, but when you're VRing a game from a flat version you don't run into such problems, some of the levels in Wrath are huge and there's so much to explore in search for secrets each level holds.
You'll get your first two weapons during the tutorial level, in the form of blades which also combines with your dash jump and your pistol, which is an okay weapon but where it really shines is from its alt fire where it shoots 3 bullets at once, which doesn't sound like much but a couple alt fire shots brings down some of the bigger tank enemies.
The first level you play should unlock a couple more weapons, Shotgun (which is something you'll probably use most of all) and the Fang Gun, which is laser accurate, so pretty quickly into the game you begin to develop a good little arsenal of gun choices.
You'll also be able to shield yourself by pressing grip on both controllers and making a cross over yourself to work as a shield, be able to slow down time while you consider your next move and jump out of the way with your dash to maybe get to some cover before handling the enemies pursuing you.
Then you have temporary power ups you'll find through level, things like an orb that protects you from enemy fire, a grenade that drags enemies into it, bottle that makes enemies fight on your side when hit being the liquid within and many more.
I've played both Quest and PSVR2 versions of the game and have over 80 hours combined in both, given the art style I couldn't really tell much of a difference in them so the quality is high across the board.
The game is £15.99, I think I was spending at least an hour in each of the 15 levels and then had the 3 bosses to fight after each world was completed, £1 an hour is a no brainer and makes the game a must buy.

reviews.review_title: Fans of DOOM will love this
reviews.review_description:
I've only finished about two levels in the game, but it's been so fantastic so far that I couldn't wait until I finished it to write my review. 

First off: 
The atmosphere is perfect. It's genuinely disturbing, and often times you'll be looking over your shoulder and listening for sounds of enemies you don't see yet. You nailed it. Not just the atmosphere, either. The designs on the weapons are also very intimidating and detailed. And the look of the graphics having that PSX aesthetic sells it even more. 

Secondly: 
I would argue these demons are a lot scarier than any of the ones in the DOOM series. And the more you progress, the more of them you meet. And in your notebook, they have really cool sketches of each one as well as their names when you encounter them. I was genuinely intimidated by every demon, and those giant floating heads with tons of teeth as well as the demons that burst out of coffins and full-speed sprint at you were terrifying. I love it. 

And secrets. My god... you even put secrets in this game, and they are so well thought out that it's impressive how fluidly you can find them if you think like you're looking for secrets in DOOM. That's phenomenal. 

My only complaint so far is the music, while excellent for atmosphere, seems to be lacking when it plays during combat. But then again, I've only beaten the snow level and am working on the swamp ruins level, so maybe there will be more intense battle music in the upcoming areas and I just haven't experienced it yet.

Overall, you don't find many VR games these days that know how to properly translate a boomer shooter from flatscreen consoles. You've earned yourself a fan. 🙏

developer_response.body: Hey Mr.Marinara, *phew*, 5-stars even after the first two levels 😂 Thank you very much for your review and we are glad that you've been enjoying the game!

reviews.review_title: Even fun for this old lady
reviews.review_description: I loved Quake back in the day and was really encouraged by the gameplay vids for Wrath. But "fast-paced" seemed a little too fast-paced (I have plenty of stress IRL, thanks). But I bit the bullet (pun intended) and I'm happy to say the easy level is just right for me. The maps are massive, the guns feel great to shoot, and everything works pretty smoothly.  The slowest movement speed is still pretty speedy, so proceed with caution if you are prone to motion sickness. Not a huge fan of the pixel art style but guess it's part of the homage. And I also have the flickering red off to the right of my vision, which I trust will be fixed. All in all I'm glad I jumped in!

oh boy, where to start..! so just for starters im only thru lvl 2 after tutorial.
so i knew nothing of this title releasing 
and when i saw team beef im sorry i was getting it, because ive never "sideloaded", or i call (jailbroken,modified)my OS's just careful
that way, but knew the type of games
they offered.--->thats the point...
im 42 years YOUNG, and old school FPS
games has always been my cloud 9, like
all of them,doom,quake, and dukeNukem

ill never forget my FIRST CPU, i was i dont know how old, wasnt wealthy and my friend had a computer and wanted me to play duke nukem but we couldnt afford a computer, it took 6 months and me and my friend found trashed/given/cheap parts from all over and built my 1st 'PC' from pretty much scratch JUST to play games such as THIS. it was like a pentium 1 like 246mhz and like 16mb RAM maybe lol i dont remember but we tied our familys phones lines up sooooooo much lol (yea youngins, nooo internet play in those times) it was the houses phone line!!   playing 4-way, 8-way multiplayer deathmath with LAN partys,ect. i used to DREAM that one day VR was a thing so that i could play them in first person. and lets see what 2015 i had the gearVR and an S7 and immediatly THIS type of game entered my mind....then. 2016,17 18 19 and barely nothing!! here lately a few good titles the are like new that takes homage to the good ol days... but not much.
ANYWAY THIS JUST LIKE THAT!   GOOD OL' OLD SCHOOL FAST KILLS, BLOODY MESS,SECRET PLACES , AND BE A BADASH
                  BUT IN VR!!!!!,, plus lol i dont find the difficulty SOOO hard like all the reviews say....guess im just old school ;)

P.S. DEVS THANK YOU SO MUCH!  DO MORE...
like.... DUKE NUKEM!!! 


(just do it)

developer_response.body: Hey Bing0Beer, those were the days! We're so glad that we can bring a piece of those carefree moments back for you to re-experience in a different medium (VR!). Thank you for taking the time to leave us such an amazing review and thank you for playing!

reviews.review_description:
As a fan of Doom and Quake there's some obvious similarities here and for standalone VR this is a pretty substantial title in terms of campaign size. It does however have some flaws that take the shine off it a bit.

Despite being ported to work in VR, the inventory system is actually pretty unintuitive. As others have mentioned there is the problem with clicking accidentally and removing weapons. This happens because the pointer defaults to the centre of the radial which empties your hand. The fact you can even have an empty hand is stupid.

You work from a central hub and go to five different areas to obtain a relic from each. I've played four of the five campaign areas and haven't had a new weapon since the start of the second. What that translates to, aside from lack of variety, is a largely empty weapon wheel. There's a separate wheel for items, most of which are useless. These two could have been combined.

Additionally, when selecting an item it empties your hand, whether you use the item or not. So you have to reselect your weapon after item use. However you can press Y to switch between your hand and the item. This should have been made to switch between the item and your weapon.

I also don't like the position of the wheel when it appears, it sits low enough to make viewing the lower area a little awkward and tilting your head down just makes it move down too. Also, visually, the sizing is IMO too small for each item.

There's no reload option, so you're forced to wait for a gun to empty then flick the chamber back, which doesn't always register. The revolver and shotgun have notably small capacity so you aren't waiting, so much as always having to do it, which is a pain in the middle of certain enemy heavy areas, especially trap rooms, and will get you killed.

Speaking of trap rooms, that clunky inventory, empty hand thing really kills the fast pace and fluidity when trying to use items and switch weapons.

In addition to the limited weapon choice you have sword blades, which are essentially useless and are primarily used as a means to perform an unnecessarily convoluted action to make it across gaps. Another thing that could have been better adapted for VR.

There are several secret areas but they don't do you any favours. You don't get upgrades, cool weapons or whatever, just little collectible armour pieces or maybe keys for a chest, and chests just drop standard in-game items.

As with the weapons, enemy type is pretty limited too, so after a while there's no real surprises. However in my last completed area there was finally a new enemy type, who appears to drop ammo for a weapon I don't have.

And speaking of dropped ammo, there's a weird glitch where it slides across the floor. At one point I had to dive into a pool to get ammo that dropped on land because it just slid all the way across the ground and kept going in the water.

There's a visual glitch which occasionally shows as some red blocks on the right side of the screen. Any attempt to bump the resolution exacerbates this quite severely.


With all that out of the way...it's a decent, lengthy shooter. It's not terrible, it's just missing refinement and some fleshing out, yet is still overall a fairly chunky offering for standalone VR. If you like this style of game you'll mostly enjoy it with some gritted teeth when the flaws catch you out, but not enough to stop you playing it.

WRATH: Aeon of Ruin VR – Brutal Edition is what happens when a classic boomer shooter crawls out of the 90s, hits the gym, and comes back swinging in full VR. This is fast, feral, and unapologetically aggressive in all the right ways. Movement feels smooth and responsive, gunplay hits with that crunchy, satisfying punch, and the moment you start circle-strafing enemies in full immersion, you realize this was always meant to be experienced like this. 

The atmosphere drips with dark fantasy grit, from eerie ruins to enemy designs that look like they crawled out of a nightmare with a personal vendetta. Weapons feel powerful and distinct, and the pacing keeps you locked in that “just one more level” spiral until suddenly you’ve lost track of time and possibly your sanity. 

This is not a watered-down port. It respects the original’s intensity while making smart adjustments for VR that elevate the experience instead of slowing it down. If you’ve ever wanted to physically dodge chaos while blasting through hordes like a caffeinated war god, this is your moment. Highly recommended. Definitely worth the value per hour (over 15 hours of gameplay)!

"WRATH: Aeon of Ruin VR - Brutal Edition isn’t just a game—it’s a love letter to classic FPS fans, elevated to perfection in VR. Flat2VR didn’t just port this; they redefined it, crafting a relentless, immersive experience that balances nostalgia (hello, Doom/Quake vibes!) with modern polish.
The story, pacing, and execution are all top-tier, but the real magic? The sheer depth of customization. Adjust movement speed, controls, and more to tailor the game to your playstyle. The guns feel weighty and satisfying, the levels are long and stressful (even on easy!), and the tension? Unrelenting.
This isn’t just another VR shooter—it’s a must-own for fans of the genre. Brutal, beautiful, and endlessly replayable. Your VR library just leveled up.

reviews.review_title: Wrath VR : the only Definitive Edition
reviews.review_description:
W:AoR- Brutal Edition is the VR conversion of the flat game made by TeamBeef, a very solid specialist in VR conversions of old-school fps.

The game can be played sitting or standing. I do not recommend this game for people prone to motion sickness, despite the accessibility options available which make the game playable, but the experience will be diminished due to its genre.

The flat game oscillates between mediocre and good (like most boomer shooters that essentially ride on nostalgia and never manage to reach the level of the games from which they draw inspiration): 
-fairly large but falsely labyrinthine levels
-layers that "weigh down" the pace (like the artifact/save system)
-poor difficulty balancing with a completely basic and stupid AI that compensates with their perverse placement/overwhelming numbers/aggressiveness/magical appearances
-mixed sensations
-sometimes strange and buggy physics, etc...

Through a (predictable) remarkable feat, Teambeef has managed to transcend the experience.
They've enhanced the gameplay by adding numerous options not present in the base version, even if it will require some muscle memory work to remember the bindings.
-They add a lot of blood (think Brutal Doom). 
-The interface is perfectly redesigned for VR. 
-The artifact system is more immersive as the weapons alt-fire.
-Offensive and defensive options are elevated by faster movements thanks to sliding and dashing with swords accessible at any time (which also allow you to block shots and attack in melee at any moment) and really impactful shooting sensations with the addition of dual wielding, bullet time, and an immersive reloading system.

Unfortunately, there are still some structural flaws inherent to the base game (cited above) which contrast with the excellence of the VR conversion and objectively, the game is a 4.
But the enjoyment, fun and immersion ultimately prevailed and managed to make me forget it in the heat of the action, so it's a 5/5 from the heart.

reviews.review_title: A Boomer Shooter Done Right in VR
reviews.review_description:
Another great one by Team Beef and Flat2VR, and a must have for fans of boomer shooters.

This game has gore,  slick movement, satisfying gunplay, and VR gestures like twisting the magnum to reload. It's satisfying to blade-dash through a swarm of enemies, and fun to run and gun while dual wielding.

developer_response.body: Hey PRVR, thanks for playing and for the kind review!

reviews.review_title: Amazing retro-shooter even better in VR
reviews.review_description:
Team Beef nailed it again. Wrath is hard, fast and brutal and if you know games like quake, doom and co... It's a must buy for VR. You get everything what you expect from an oldschool shooter: search for keycards, very high difficulty, very fast paced action, alot of weapons and it's brutal !
Flat2VR studios releases really top notch games from any genre you can think of and it's a no brainer to buy them day1 IMO. I'm really glad that Team Beef joined them too, cause they only produce top notch "ports" too.

So, if you like shooters just buy it.

reviews.review_title: What The Quest Has Been Waiting For
reviews.review_description:
Waited since the Quest 1 to get an official Doom-alike release, and finally here it is!
Brilliant gameplay and lovely expansive environments, with so much atmosphere and a retro aesthetic, but with modern visual upgrades like great lighting and effects.
This is a proper game, and pretty tough, but never in a way that feels unfair. You've just got to be on your toes!
Everyone even remotely into shooters should be getting this. It's a must-have. Faultless.

reviews.review_title: 3D Realms Hardcore FPS Comes to VR
reviews.review_description: Wrath VR takes the fast paced 90s style FPS and brings it into the realm of VR. Allowing you to experience the thrill of gunning down enemies with a wide arsenal all with the interactivity and immersion one would expect from VR. All with 4.99 knocked off the top. I had a great time with the game, especially once I cranked up the speed slider and began experimenting between Akimbo and Two Handed combat. Definitely a strong recommendation for anyone wanting an intense single player shooter experience.

reviews.review_title: A PERFECT Boomer Shooter in VR
reviews.review_description:
Wrath is a fantastic boomer shooter that has been adapted brilliantly for VR! Every action feels so natural, making combat incredibly fluid as you move between guns and power-ups to deal with a wide variety of enemies. Many features surpass their inspiration (especially the artefact that tells you which direction to go, as I often find myself getting lost in games like Quake haha). Could not recommend more!

There are only minor issues, such as certain levels in the final act crashing on occasion and the lack of checkpoints on the second boss being slightly tedious, but otherwise runs incredibly and will only get better with time!

Can't wait to replay on Outlander difficulty as I'm really feeling up for a challenge, five stars well deserved!

I've owned a quest 3 for a year now. This is my only review I've written since then and I own 20 games with no review period. I Was so shocked to see the 3D Realms logo and team beef together. Felt like a kid playing duke nukem 3d for the first time. This is such a success that maybe a duke nukem, tomb raider or a wolfeinstien is possible. I've played the versions from what I've found on my own that is from Team Beef. Hopefully we can have some sort of version of those games here including a Zelda kind of game. Please keep up the good work.

Wrath: Aeon of Ruin is a fun boomer shooter that was originally a flat-screen version. The developers had to add new mechanics for VR, like having to swing your sword, since in the original, you could only dash forward with it. The guns are nice to shoot, and it feels awesome sliding across with either a pistol or shotgun. Having the ability to time stop makes you feel like John Wick with precision shots to enemies, blowing out their brains. The world has massive, non-linear levels, allowing you to explore in a couple of ways. If you keep dying in one area and want to save your progress within the level, you'll need a skull and to visit a shrine. This prevents you from spam-saving all the time and makes you think strategically about when to save during the level, as you can find at least one throughout the levels. Also, after finishing a level, you return to a massive hub, which is also non-linear, being able to choose any level you want to do in the beginning.

In conclusion, if you would want an "Ultrakill" type of game, this would be it since it's not fast-paced, but this game focuses on exploration rather than running through levels, kicking everything, and calling it a day.

Games like this adds more value to the Meta platform. Wrath looks, feels and plays great on the Quest 3…it also works well with bhaptics. Intuitive controls, lots of guns and enemies! If you like Quake, Doom, Hexen or even Boltgun , you will like this!

reviews.review_title: Another great Team Beef VR port!
reviews.review_description:
Yes it's a low poly 'boomer shooter' ported to VR but Team Beef has done an awesome job in doing it. 

Having played a lot of both the OG flatscreen version now and the VR Brutal Edition, I think one of the best features in the game (besides playing in full-scale VR) is the dual wielding, and not only can you dual wield the same weapon, you can do one of each.
Like have the blade or ruination in your hand to use for melee attacks when using ranged gun in the other hand for missed shots or to finish off enemies.  Also another great feature that was added was the rechargeable slowmo 'bullet time' you can use to get out of tight spots when fighting a lot of enemies at once.

Also I like how the gameplay isn't a typical corridor boomer shooter, each stage has wide open areas with multiple paths and it even has a pathfinding artifact you can use for a hint on where to go if you get lost (that feature was not in the OG flatscreen version.)

The gameplay also allows you to go speed running guns blazing or you can be more methodical in saving ammo and attacking enemies and take your time going through the level.

The room scale of the game is awesome in VR as well. Enemies like the Executioner and the flying Heretic are terrifying when you encounter them in VR. 

Overall this is a great VR shooter that feels like a native VR game and is a lot of fun to play. Recommended.

Note:  I received a copy of this game for pre-release playtesting but it didn't effect the review or opinion of the game.

LOVE, LOVE, LOVE this game, but theres a glitch where you cant activate floor switches...you have to creep up slowly to make it highlight to let you hit X to activate it. Now Im on a later level where it wont highlight at all. Please patch this so I can continue to blast monsters.

developer_response.body:
Hi Rojman, 
It activates by head gaze so looking directly at it when close enough should do. However we'll improve the area to focus on in the next patch. Thanks for your feedback. Team Beef

reviews.review_description:
Just finished it. The game is quite lengthy (took me about 12 hours) but you won't be bored. Plenty of guns( all with alternative fire) , boosting items and lots of fast paced, gory and brutal action. The graphics, music and sound effects are awesome too. We need more games like this on Quest 3.
Must buy for every fan of shooters.

I though I'm pretty good at those, I'm doing great in Roboquest and other similar titles but this has humbled me!
I recommend starting on easy and making use of the slow-mo a lot

But when You're not overwhelmed? It feels great to be doing good!

The reload gestures can be a bit wonky with the detection (or it's my frantic movements when panicking), and it's pretty difficult to change weapons quickly, you can't change the weapons in both hands at the same time, and I'd say the music is too lowkey and chill, as opposed to f.e. a Doom soundtrack

A must for all old school video game players, I will admit it took a while for me to really get to grips with the game, no explanation where to find you soul tethers, and power ups, but once you work out where everything think is, it’s carnage in a good way. 
Only downside is the left movement stick, this also contains your weapons, so sometimes whilst running with a gun, you can accidentally click & your gun goes back into the hidden slot, in the heat of battle this can be annoying.
You can’t fault the action though, it’s full on brutal chaos, monsters in every direction, take your time and learn the basics & you will have an amazing time .
Weak stomachs need not apply, this is full on madness only the strongest can survive.

This game is a great old school shooter. Perfect blend of quake atmosphere with doom style hordes and types of enemies. Its easily worth $20 and better than amidevil vr. 

Only reason why its not 5 stars for me is a controll issue that probably affects me more than others. To select a weapon for your off hand you click the thumb stick and a wheel pulls up. I didn't have an issue with this until I came across a horrifying open mouthed creature that runs straight at you screaming. During my panic to evade from this truly nightmarish creature I inadvertently press down on the left stick which automatically disarms that hand. So when trying to kill that screaming monstrosity im doing it one handed, lol.

Small complaint but I keep doing it so often I sometimes end my games session early due to frustration. 

But besides that I 100% recommend this game and can say its better than every other "boomer" or "old school" shooter natively on quest. 

Im playing on a quest 2 and it looks and runs great! Curious how much better on a 3? I so appreciate the developers making it available for both headsets. I can afford $20 but not $250 plus to upgrade.......unfortunately.

Its a very well put together game, and though it says standing only, it plays fine seated. Ocarina of time is one of my favourite all time games but trying to play it now is not a great experience. Same thing with this - its a good game but the old run around and shoot everything formula is just showing its age too much to keep me engaged. Something like Bulletstorm has the same kind of action but with more meat on its bones

This is insane in a good way intense, fast-paced experience that feels fantastic as a great nod to Doom and Quake old school style. The combat is satisfying, the action is relentless, and the immersion pulls you right into the chaos. The controls feel responsive and natural, making every fight feel skill-based and rewarding.

There’s a great sense of progression, and the gameplay keeps you coming back for “just one more go.” Visually it looks great in VR, and the atmosphere really adds to the tension. If you enjoy action-packed VR games with challenge, excitement and loads of replay value then this is it.  But be advised you do need to have great VR legs or be playing in shorter bouts.

Great boomer shooter! (doom/quake style) We need more ports like this. Great vr games don't have to be graphically impressive because the key is immersion and fun! And this flat port of a quake inspired game that came out just a couple years ago does just that. Great speed and line up of weapons with good implementation of vr mechanics. No surprise seeing team beef work on this and this seems to be their specialty! And teaming up with flat to vr another very reliable team doing flat to vr ports knocks another out the park with a game that's better in vr than on flat! (Check out their port robo quest)Love the idea of porting games of similar graphical styles. Seems like games from 2000 to 2010 level graphics work well in VR. Doom 3 (thanks team beef) resident evil 4 and now this modern spin with Wrath on it are great examples. Hope to see other great games. Give these two teams more opportunities to bring games to VR because they do fantastic jobs with their ports! 

Side note, a game like Wrath/Doom/Quake though is not for the newbies of vr due to its speed. You really have to build up your VR legs first to be able to take on such games. But once you do it with some smaller experiences, games like this are amazing to play. I highly suggest this to anybody who enjoys high-speed action!

reviews.review_title: Great if you like original doom
reviews.review_description: It's a solid boomer-shooter in VR. Maps are okay, the enemies are plenty, guns play okay. Keep in mind that the difficulty is rather high, but easy mode is doable. As for the problems, I belive the saving system is not good - you get skulls that let you save, but tbh, these all could be just an autosave locations. Maps are also a tad big.

reviews.review_title: I really want to enjoy this game, but…
reviews.review_description: This is not normally my type of game, but it seemed interesting sort of as an amalgamation of games from my childhood. Quake was mythic for me because Trent Reznor did the music and sound design but my computer was not fast enough to play it and only experienced it through small bursts on friend’s computers. Loved Duke Nukem 3D, and Doom 1 & 2. I love the design of Wrath, the size of levels and the twisted design of the enemies and locations. Without those games mentioned, I don’t think this game would exist, but they do good job of making this feel like a love letter to those games, instead of some copy/paste derivative. All of this makes me sad because the game is completely unplayable for me. The weapon wheel that pops up when you click your stick, slows down time, allowing you to switch your weapons on the fly, and that decision seems completely boneheaded to me, and I’m a little amazed this didn’t get resolved during play testing. I really need to focus to be able to use the left stick for movement without accidentally clicking it. I’ve had some success of being aware of that but anytime I get surprised by an enemy, or get swarmed, it’s unavoidable. The game gets intense and then I’m constantly unequipping my weapon and I have no idea why one of the buttons on the left controller weren’t utilized when one of those buttons isn’t even assigned to anything. Just a dead button. It’s not really an issue for the right controller, as that is not used for locomotion. But for a game this fast paced and intense, it kills all rhythm when that happens. I can’t tell you how many times I have huge enemies 10 feet in front of me, and I raise my shotgun and then opening the weapon wheel, unequipping, then re-equipping, and losing all momentum. Then it just becomes a very aggravating way to play this game. It is simply unplayable for me.

Fun intense fps vr action game. It feels like quake but made into VR. the levels are pretty big to explorer and collect the items. It is a bit of a learning curve collecting items and using them. Or you csn have fun running around shooting the enemies for fun. The movement and game play is smooth. 2nd and 3rd times playing it I felt way more comfortable playing than the first. I like how I catching how fast I move and the smooth turning from the default/snap turning. Sound effects enhancing the game play. You can tell a lot of detail and time went into this game. The underwater movements can get your heart beating also collecting an item and only having 1 health left. You can put on a mask that extends your time under water or be a risk taker like me and figure that out after trying without it.

This is an amazing port of the original. I have never played the original but if its even half as good as this we are all winning by playing it in VR. The game hits with all the nostalgia of Boomer shooters and having it in VR makes it that much more fun. Combat is a bit difficult to master at first but once you get the hang of it, its off the the races. Using different combos with the acquired weapons really lets you rip through enemies in your own creative way. Graphics are like you would expect, but drops you right into a time machine of nostalgia. Enemies are all uniquely designed and until you learn what each type does, watch out. Dying in this game is no joke and if you havent used your save item, your screwed. None the less plenty of game here for anyone who likes dungeon crawlers and first person shooters. Well worth the purchase to support Team Beef and their efforts as well.

reviews.review_title: One for those who love boomer shooter
reviews.review_description: If you like boomer shooter, you will love this. From art to style, this feels reminiscent to games like original doom and quake. The shooting feels great and reloading is satisfying in style to arcade shooter by just flicking the gun. If i have to nitpick, this feels more like a mod than a port. This is due to lack of interaction with the virtual world, example, to flick a switch/lever stand in front of it and press A. A locked door? Find the key then press A in front of the lock door. Open a treasure chest? Press A, switch weapon? Press Y to open weapon wheel. Even then, i still enjoy the game quite a bit  and it's worth a  purchase

reviews.review_title: A good game, elevated by VR
reviews.review_description:
Solid VR conversion that personally improves the original in major ways.
Performance and visuals are excellent, and the fundamental gameplay loop translates beautifully to VR.
What really sticks out is that the VR version isn't just a surface level port, it adds a few new features like slow-mode, reloads and duel-wielding that make it feel more transformative.
If you enjoyed the original flat game, or boomer shooters in general, you should absolutely revisit it in VR.

Old school boomer shooters were never my thing, but as any VR gamer will tell you, VR makes everything BETTER!  What Team Beef and Flat2VR Studios have done is take a game that I would not normally be interested in and delivered a high-octane, fast-paced shooter that I can really sink my teeth into!  Speaking of teeth, the Fangspitter is probably my favourite weapon in the game.  It’s a SMG style gun that just keeps firing until you run out of ammo, but even if you do, then just shoot some more enemies in the face till they explode and get some more!

The game has 3 hub areas, each with 5 levels which you can do in any order, and epic boss fights, you really are getting your money's worth with the playtime.  I was averaging around 45 minutes to an hour on each because the levels are huge, each filled with hidden secrets to find in the hard-to-reach areas.

With a great variety of weapons, different artifacts that grant temporary defensive or offensive abilities, you can really find the approach that matches your play style.  There’s even a ‘bullet time’ ability that makes you feel awesome!  Whether you choose to take it cautiously or just run in guns blazing, you’re going to have a great experience regardless!

Wrath definitely gets a MUST BUY from me!
😁✌️☮️❤️
You can also check out my first impressions gameplay on my channel!

Unfortunately, I'm the Llama. I never played, or frankly even heard of, this game. However, if you know Doom or Quake you'll recognize it immediately. I have dabbled in a few Boomer Shooters and I played the originals back when they came out. I'll admit that I'm not great at them so I started this one on Easy. So far it's still a challenge and I've heard from other players that if you want the "Brutal" from the title, you can have it.

The graphics are good, given the retro style it's going for. There are a lot of greys and browns, but it does mix it up. This is not in the day glow retro style that some games go for, but you can tell that from the screenshots. Textures are blocky but that also means they aren't filtered so graphics are sharp. Geometry is simplified, but not flat or uninteresting. There's plenty of cool stuff to see here, but it's stylized. Having it and seeing it in VR is, of course, a huge boost to visual interest.

Speaking of VR, they have added some nice enhancements. You reload with a gesture rather than a button press. It's not a weapon reload sequence, but that would *not* be a boon in this game. It adds dual wielding, and you can use the selection wheel to pick something different for each hand. It's one of the best things about the game. My favorite combo so far is the Fang Spitter in my right and the Shotgun in my left. You can also instantly grab your blades with the grip button, so you've really got options! There's also slow mode and a bunch of different artifacts to play with, which I probably should have used way more than I did. I tend to horde items until I need them but I need to get over that. Those intense moments come quite frequently! 

At first you just get a few zombies to kill and that gave me a false sense of security. Then the game throws in a couple of stronger shotgun enemies, and those aren't too bad either. You just need to be a bit more careful. Then I got what I thought was a mini boss, which was still manageable. Then I found out they weren't mini-bosses. Soon, they and similar high level enemies started popping up in multiples. It gets frantic. The different enemies all behave differently and require a different approach. Those stupid flying wasp/pod things are so annoying! They aren't much of a threat (unless in mass), but they will keep your eyes on the skies.

I got the relic! I made it through! That one level took me a few hours, but I was slow and overly defensive. I think there are 15 levels, so this might take me a while. Other people are clearing the levels much faster, but even so, if you are looking for a game that you won't finish in a couple of afternoons, here you go!

I'm enjoying the game, but I do have a couple of quibbles. Different weapons reload differently and it's really easy to miss a reload. Also, you can't preemptively reload. I'm not completely sure this is a downside because I suspect skill will fix it and it does add tension and desperation when you're in a fight and you're out of bullets. My favorite way to die was right in the face of an enemy flicking my weapon, failing, and getting murdered. My biggest issue is the weapon selection remapping. Pressing the joystick clears your weapon and I want a way to turn that off. I think they did this because you need a free hand to trigger the alternate fire on the guns, but I clear my guns accidentally all the time.

In short, I highly recommend it! I received this game for free as a play tester and I've already seen some plans for the first patch. If they can fix the accidental weapon clears, I think my score will change to a 5!

Wrath Aeon of ruin is honestly a really solid package, and is worth your time.

First off, it's a VR conversion done by the *legends* at Flat2VR studios and Team Beef. They have taken a flatscreen game and made it feel like a native VR title. The flatscreen version was released a couple years ago, and is a modern take on retro shooters like Doom and Quake. It's got a very crunchy art style, and feels like a better version of an older game.

Wrath runs incredibly well. Even with the fast/smooth movement I was engaging in (there are movement/comfort options like snap turn, movement speed adjustment, etc), my typical play session was about 1.5 hours, but at times I played around two hours, and it always felt comfortable. I gave the devs feedback that the movement and gameplay was so nausea-free that they could pull off a VR port of Aliens vs Predator without issue!

There are a wide variety of unique weapons, and they're all very satisfying. You can dual wield whatever combo you want, and even two-hand grip weapons as well, with a special alt-fire option while two-handing it. There's a giant wrist blade you can easily pull out for each hand, and you can charge the blade to dash across gaps and through enemies.

The world design has great vibes! I'm only 1/3 though the game, and I've already encountered a realm that has a moderate touch of *Bloodborne* vibes. The music is cool and haunting, though one of my only two criticisms is that the very *metal* startup music isn't really reflective of the music playing during the game from what I've seen (it's usually haunted ambiance). 

The other criticism I have is that, since each hand has its own weapon wheel that you pull up by clicking/holding in the analog/thumbstick, I sometimes accidentally put my left hand weapon away during intense combat, because I use the left controller analog stick to move through the world. BUT I have provided this feedback directly to the devs, and I hear they are working on this issue.

As I've been playtesting the game, I got it for free. But I've played roughly the first third of Wrath three times now (there are 15 levels, and each take about an hour), and I enjoyed it so much that I'm going to finish the entire game just for fun, even after many hours of playtesting. It's a mark of quality that I still want to play after that many hours of replaying it.

I think Wrath Aeon of Ruin is a very good time, and it genuinely stands out amongst the quest library. I highly recommend checking it out!

reviews.review_title: Like the OG Doom, but on steroids
reviews.review_description: I remember the dozens of hours and countless nights way back in the day when the original Doom came out on PC. We played that game nonstop cuz of how awesome it was. Wrath Aeon of Ruin brings back that same nostalgia with the same graphics style and gameplay but cranks it up the adrenaline with more action, more weapons, more enemies and nonstop fast paced action that begs me to crank up the 80s rock music while playing. That might all sound like an exaggeration but it’s not. This game is a blast! And the extendable arm blades makes you feel like an unstoppable old school version of Wolverine. Rampaging fun!

I've followed the team behind this port for quite some time and assume based on others' reviews that this is likely an isolated case, but I currently can't play the game on my Quest Pro due to a strange flickering issue every few seconds (including odd artifacts in my peripheral vision). My headset's up to date and no other apps behave like this, but I trust that this will be patched in the future and will leave an updated review to reflect that. 

Looking forward to enjoying the game! :)

developer_response.body: Hi Gogolta, did this not improve with the update we dropped end of last week? 1.0.0010? The review says you haven't tried that one as yet so hopeful it fixes the issue. If its still a problem please let us know and we'll look further. Unfortunately no one in the team with a Quest Pro, but we will find someone to test if not. Team Beef

reviews.review_description:
If you’re looking for a true challenge, this game absolutely delivers. Even on easy mode, I found it tough, but to be fair, I’m not the best at fast-paced shooters, but the difficulty never felt unfair, and it kept me coming back.

The game offers a great variety of environments to explore, along with plenty of weapons to experiment with and enemies to take down. The sound design really stands out. You can clearly hear where enemies are coming from, which adds a lot to the intensity and immersion.

Progression is entirely earned. Nothing is handed to you, you have to work for every upgrade and advancement. The map system is also well done, helping you track your path as you explore, collect keys, and unlock new areas.

I especially liked the use of portals, which add both convenience and a fun twist to navigation and combat.

Make no mistake, this game is difficult. But pushing through those tough moments and finally overcoming them is incredibly rewarding. If you enjoy games that test your skill and persistence, this one is definitely worth your time.

I got a copy of this for testing, and I'm certainly not the target audience (though I'm a huge fan of classic games and difficulty), but man did I have a blast with this one.  I went in not expecting a lot and ended up blown away by the depth of it.  This stays true to the classic game (and games like it, such as Quake) while having a fresh spin for VR gameplay.  I can easily recommend this to anyone who enjoys a good "Boomer Shooter" with lots of exploration and challenging gameplay to keep you interested, and intuitive VR controls.  Another home run from Team Beef and Flat2VR.  If it looks interesting to you at all, just get it.

reviews.review_title: Just how I imagined it back in the day
reviews.review_description: I played a lot of Doom and Quake when they came out and always imagined back then what it would be like to be in a game like that in VR (1997 first time trying VR). Now I know, and it's even better than I dreamed of. This is a pure nostalgia boomer shooter, but even more elevated by the careful adjustments and improvements made to the VR version, making it even better than the flat version. Minor things will surely be ironed out, but be aware that even on the easiest settings this is still a hard core game and you will die a lot. Make sure to use the save features! Also supports my bHaptics vest, which is an added bonus if you have one

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review QuinrodD! Very glad you are enjoying it. Always nice to find a fellow bHaptics player. We really love the punch it packs on Wrath.

reviews.review_title: A New Spin On A Classic!! ⭐️
reviews.review_description:
This game is honestly amazing and shouldn’t be slept on! The graphics remind me of compound VR which already did a great job with the pixelated art style and I’m glad to see more games adopt it! The gameplay left me hooked for hours and nothing ever felt repetitive I felt excited to get back in after my headset died! I found little to no bugs which left me immersed the whole time with nothing ever holding me back or feeling confused. Combat felt satisfying with each slice making everything you do feel perfect!

Don’t sleep on Flat2VR Buy now!!!

What a blast!  What I thought was sort of a meh boomer shooter is now an insane pile of shooting, blood, and difficulty.  The VR implementation here is brilliant, from the help getting acquainted with the controls, to quick reloading and quick access to blades and abilities helped by the ability to slow time.  No VR battles have come close to the intensity and adrenaline on show here.  The only negative thing I can think of is the difficulty level, but I also just suck at FPS, and it is the whole point of this game.  You have to be good at dealing with a giant number of enemies, but what they've done with the controls makes that possible in such a satisfying way!

10/10 VR must own.  Even if you thought the flat or previous VR iteration was meh.  I got this free from supporting Team Beef, but decided to gift it to a friend to help support the devs.  They deserve it!  What are you doing reading, go play it!

reviews.review_title: Perfect in vr treat yourself today
reviews.review_description: WRATH is the game you've been waiting for if you enjoyed any boomer shooters in the past. Do yourself a favour and grab this game. Controls are fantastic all the guns feel amazing to use the fact you can akimbo all the guns yes even akimbo shotguns is awesome each weapon has a secondary fire aswell. This game has massive levels and tons of secrets to find

Haven't played the flatscreen verison of Wrath and I don't think I need to. This port to VR is a Rock Solid Boomer Shooter, the reloading pairs well with the fast paced gunplay. Although I do think the enemy variety is lacking, but the unique maps definitely make up for it.

My back was sore after playing this game - 8/10

You guys replied faster than my siblings ever do 😭 that’s a 5⭐ in my book already

Thanks for the response, appreciate it.

Good to hear the audio bug is being looked into, and the option to switch back to the original blade sounds perfect.

Just two quick things:

There’s basically no music during gameplay — it would be awesome if combat music could kick in during fights. Something like rock would honestly go hard in VR and make the action feel way more intense.

And the blade feels a bit underpowered right now. I know it’s based on the original flat screen balance, but in VR it doesn’t feel as rewarding. If melee could one or two-shot enemies, it would make close combat way more satisfying and addictive … higher risk, higher reward,… running in and shredding enemies would feel amazing 😅

Other than that, I’m really enjoying it so far. please keep it coming :)

developer_response.body:
Hey 7aki, thank you for your review. We hear you on your points and wanted to mention that we are aware of the audio bug and are actively looking into it. We are also working on having including an option for players to switch back to the original blade and we are aiming to have it included in the next update. If you happen to find any other issues while playing, we'd appreciate it if you could leave your feedback and any video evidences gathered here: https://wrath-labs.feedbear.com/boards/livebuild

Thank you for playing!

reviews.review_description: Wrath captures everything great about old school shooters, from fast, frantic combat to large levels and pixelated gore, all while running buttery smooth the whole time. The levels feel huge and are filled with keys, chests to unlock, doors to open, unlockable guns and secrets scattered everywhere. Enemies can feel overwhelming at times, but you can cut through them with a mix of guns and blades that you can dual wield. Some weapons even have alt fire modes for when you want to be extra spicy. It takes a minute to feel comfortable with the VR controls, but once it clicks, it really clicks. Learning how to mix offense with defensive tools like bullet time, blocking, and potions makes the combat feel fluid. It even has native support for bHaptics. So if you have a vest it will really enhance the experience since you feel every shot and attack. It can be tough, especially on higher difficulties, but that challenge is part of what makes it so satisfying.

This is a nice brutal vr game where you fight demons , constantly in action with in the middle graphics that are perfect and I have been waiting for this game to be released on Vr ever since I got the ps5 version but has unique gameplay that keeps you playing and just should be way more popular and should be on horizon plus🙏😃

Devs...my guys...the weapon select using the thumb stick press is the worst idea. Makes it unplayable for me. You're in a heated battle and surprise your hands are empty. This is a must fix. I'm trying to get into the game but its too aggravating to deal with.

reviews.review_title: Brutal Boomer Shooter Keeping VR Alive
reviews.review_description:
Flat2VR Studios and Team Beef took an already insanely great game, and cranked it up to 11 with this VR port. Wrath already was known for being awesome, but it truly shines in VR and runs extremely well. 

The game feels made for VR instead of just a simple port, and being able to live and breathe in the world makes even turn and shot become much more realistic. It's chaotic and blisteringly brutal and difficult, but in the best ways possible. This is for true hardcore gamers and it shows. 

Performance on the Quest headset was perfect, I never experienced any slowdown or hitches. The game's checkpoints are few and far between so make sure you have a fully charged battery. 

If you love games like Doom, or Quake... this brings back the Quake vibes like nothing else out there. It's dark, dreary, gritty and just insanely well made from the ground up for VR.

reviews.review_title: Action thriller game with Doom vibes
reviews.review_description: This game has a hint of doom vibes with the gory kills and the picking up ammo and armor from the ground. Visually it’s okay with the environments pretty expansive but generically look the same with some are very unique. Like the graveyard. It’s a learning curve having to use the joystick to switch weapons. It also gets in the way as you can easily press down on the joy sticks while running from enemies and you either open up the inventory with the left joystick or activate the slow motion with the right. It also is confusing when there’s doors to unlock but there’s no visual instruction on what to do which is pressing x. This made me think I was missing something and kept restarting till finally I just pressed x on a whim. Calling this experience Vr is very minimal as you don’t grab or interact by hands. Just by buttons. Your hands of course is just for combat. The blade dash is pretty cool and the enemy can get scary but I just don’t know if there’s enough to justify a $16 price tag with bare minimal narrating and lack of a story. It’s a generally basic fun game if just looking for gory doom like action.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review. I'll respond to your points:

We're thinking about an alternate for the weapon wheels. There's not a lot of buttons to go around on the Quest controllers. But we'll work on an alternative in the first rounds of feedback. 

For doors there is a visual indicator, its just on your hands, so if you play with your hands down it might be missed. Always a balance as also spawning these too much in the user's view and it can be distracting. We'll have another look and find a better balance. 

VR element we've focused on the main part of the game, making the gunplay as satisfying as possible and giving as many different ways to play, features  and tactics as possible. We felt this was far more important than the occasional switch or chest which would have felt a bit tacked onto the original flatscreen game. 

Lastly I will say players will find a hugely lengthy campaign, 3 boss fights a ton of levels and atmospheres and a good 15 hours to complete (even on easy). The story elements can be found by secrets and in World notes as well as Shepherd's narration. But you are entirely correct. This is a Doom / Quake style boomer shooter. We wouldn't want to pretend its anything but that. If people enjoy shredding through hundreds of enemies thrown at them over a variety of Worlds, this is the game.

reviews.review_description: I absolutely love this game, I’m still playing it right now. It’s a great first person shooter with every stage having its own unique puzzle. I definitely recommend getting this game!
If you played and enjoyed any of the above mentioned games back in the day, Wrath VR brings the magic! Buy it, it’s well worth it 👍

developer_response.body: Hey CURTROCK, thank you for playing and for taking the time to write us a review!

reviews.review_description: Little frustrating at times as game progresses but I am fully enjoying the game. Great job once again Team Beef/Flat2VR.

I had an amazing time playing and shooting monsters. The graphics were indeed good and gives off that nostalgia. I did enjoy  exploring around as well. Recommend to any friend ￼that loves this type of genre.￼

This game is exactly what I've been waiting for. A fast-paced, brutal shooter with tons of weapons. Playing it reminds me of when I first played Doom as a kid. Tons of enemies, powerful weapons, and total annihilation... I recommend it to everyone.

Team Beef and 3D realms - what a great collaboration! I wonder if Team Beef could team up with night dive studios and bring power slave to Quest? 

Wrath is excellent, it's THE best way to play this game

Outstanding game. It has the vibes of Quake 1 with a modern flash. i am finally able to give my money to Team Beef, after Awesome MODs of classic shooters. Hope they get hired to convert more Boomer shooters in the future.
